question: [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator call setIsAuthorized(user, false, user) under the precondition that isAuthorized[user][operator] == true, triggering the operator revoking the user's own self-authorization (isAuthorized[user][user] = false), violating the invariant that a user must always be able to act on their own behalf (msg.sender == onBehalf check is separate from isAuthorized), causing scoped impact: user's self-authorization is revoked but user can still act directly via msg.sender check, so no direct loss but integration contracts relying on isAuthorized[user][user] may break? Proof idea: operator calls setIsAuthorized(user, false, user), assert isAuthorized[user][user] == false but user can still call withdraw() directly.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator call setIsAuthorized(operator, false, user) under the precondition that isAuthorized[user][operator] == true, triggering the operator revoking their own authorization, violating the invariant that authorization changes must be intentional by the authorizer, causing scoped impact: operator can grief themselves or be used in a griefing attack where a malicious sub-operator revokes the legitimate operator's access? Proof idea: operator calls setIsAuthorized(operator, false, user), assert isAuthorized[user][operator] == false, operator can no longer act on user's behalf.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator call setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, user) under the precondition that isAuthorized[user][operator] == true and user is a maker with active offers in group, triggering cancellation of all of user's offers in that group, violating the invariant that offer cancellation must be authorized by the maker, causing scoped impact: operator can cancel all of user's maker offers, preventing them from earning yield as a lender? Proof idea: user signs offers in group G, operator calls setConsumed(G, type(uint256).max, user), attempt to fill user's offer and assert revert with ConsumedUnits().

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized (EcrecoverAuthorizer)] Can an attacker replay an EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized signature on a different chain under the precondition that EcrecoverAuthorizer uses block.chainid (not INITIAL_CHAIN_ID) for domain separation while Midnight uses INITIAL_CHAIN_ID for market IDs, triggering a valid authorization on a forked chain where block.chainid changed but INITIAL_CHAIN_ID did not, violating the invariant that signatures must not be replayable across chains, causing scoped impact: attacker replays authorization signature on fork to gain control over user's positions? Proof idea: simulate hard fork (change block.chainid), replay old EcrecoverAuthorizer signature, assert it succeeds on fork but should not.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized (EcrecoverAuthorizer)] Can an already-authorized operator submit an EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized call signed by themselves (not the authorizer) under the precondition that IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).isAuthorized(authorization.authorizer, signer) == true, triggering a valid authorization change where the operator grants or revokes authorizations on behalf of the authorizer without the authorizer's direct signature, violating the invariant that authorization changes must be signed by the authorizer or their delegate, causing scoped impact: operator can grant attacker full control over authorizer's positions? Proof idea: user authorizes operator in Midnight, operator signs Authorization struct for user, call EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized, assert success and isAuthorized[user][attacker] == true.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized (EcrecoverAuthorizer)] Can an attacker submit an EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized call with a valid signature but nonce == 0 after the authorizer's nonce has already been incremented past 0, under the precondition that nonce[authorizer] > 0, triggering a revert with InvalidNonce(), violating the invariant that nonce must exactly match current value, causing scoped impact: DoS of legitimate authorization updates if attacker front-runs with a nonce-consuming call? Proof idea: front-run user's setIsAuthorized call with same nonce, assert user's call reverts with InvalidNonce().

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker use multicall to batch setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, victim) + take(offer, ..., taker=victim) in a single transaction under the precondition that attacker is already authorized by victim (isAuthorized[victim][attacker] == true), triggering a take on victim's behalf within the same multicall that granted the authorization, violating the invariant that authorization grants must not be composable with immediate position changes in the same atomic call, causing scoped impact: attacker atomically authorizes themselves and drains victim's collateral? Proof idea: multicall([setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, victim), withdrawCollateral(market, 0, maxCollateral, victim, attacker)]) and assert collateral transferred.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee + take] Can feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newFee) during the execution of a take() callback (onBuy/onSell) under the precondition that the callback contract is the feeSetter or calls feeSetter, triggering a settlement fee change mid-take that affects the buyerAssets/sellerAssets already computed before the callback, violating the invariant that settlement fee must be consistent within a single take() execution, causing scoped impact: fee accounting desync between computed buyerAssets-sellerAssets spread and claimableSettlementFee increment? Proof idea: callback calls setMarketSettlementFee, assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] after take equals pre-take value plus (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) computed with original fee.

[File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing + take] Can tickSpacingSetter call setMarketTickSpacing(id, newSpacing) during a take() callback under the precondition that the callback contract is the tickSpacingSetter, triggering a tick spacing change after the tick validity check (require(offer.tick % _marketState.tickSpacing == 0)) but before the take completes, violating the invariant that tick validity must be consistent throughout a single take() execution, causing scoped impact: offer filled at a tick that is invalid under the new spacing, corrupting the tick grid? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setFeeClaimer + claimSettlementFee] Can roleSetter call setFeeClaimer(newClaimer) immediately after a large take() accrues settlement fees, under the precondition that the old feeClaimer has not yet called claimSettlementFee, triggering the old claimer losing all unclaimed fees (per the comment 'When the claimer is set, the old claimer loses the unclaimed fees'), violating the invariant that accrued fees must be claimable by the entity that earned the right to claim them, causing scoped impact: permanent loss of settlement fees for the displaced claimer? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setFeeSetter] Can roleSetter call setFeeSetter(address(0)) under the precondition that there are markets with non-zero settlement fees that need future adjustment, triggering permanent disabling of all fee setter functions (setMarketSettlementFee, setDefaultSettlementFee, setMarketContinuousFee, setDefaultContinuousFee), violating the invariant that fee parameters must remain adjustable by the protocol, causing scoped impact: permanent freeze of fee governance, settlement fees locked at current values forever? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setFeeClaimer] Can roleSetter call setFeeClaimer(address(0)) under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[token] > 0 and continuousFeeCredit > 0 for active markets, triggering permanent disabling of claimSettlementFee and claimContinuousFee (require(msg.sender == feeClaimer) with feeClaimer == address(0) means only address(0) can claim), violating the invariant that accrued fees must be claimable, causing scoped impact: all accrued settlement and continuous fees permanently frozen in the contract? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee] Can feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 0, MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_0_DAYS) (index=0, post-maturity fee) to set the post-maturity settlement fee to its maximum (0.000014e18 = 0.14 bps) under the precondition that the market is post-maturity and has resting sell offers with price < MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_0_DAYS, triggering implicit cancellation of those low-price offers (take reverts with 'Taking buy offers with price < settlement fee will revert'), violating the invariant that existing offers must not be implicitly cancelled by fee changes, causing scoped impact: lenders with resting sell offers at low prices cannot unwind post-maturity? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee] Can feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, 0) for all 7 breakpoints under the precondition that the market has active trading, triggering zero settlement fees for all TTM ranges, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be non-negative and within protocol-defined bounds (0 is technically allowed), causing scoped impact: protocol earns zero settlement fees, claimableSettlementFee never increases, fee claimer receives nothing? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: touchMarket (market parameter setter)] Can any unprivileged user call touchMarket(market) with a market where collateralParams[i].maxLif is set to maxLif(lltv, LIQUIDATION_CURSOR_LOW) for one collateral and maxLif(lltv, LIQUIDATION_CURSOR_HIGH) for another under the precondition that both are valid per the require check, triggering a market with mixed liquidation cursor values across collaterals, violating the invariant that liquidation incentive must be consistent within a market, causing scoped impact: liquidators can selectively choose collaterals with higher LIF, extracting more value than intended? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: touchMarket (market parameter setter)] Can any unprivileged user call touchMarket(market) with rcfThreshold == 0 under the precondition that the market has LLTV < WAD, triggering a market where the RCF dust check is always satisfied (0 < rcfThreshold is always false when rcfThreshold=0, meaning the RCF is always active), violating the invariant that small collateral positions should allow full liquidation to avoid dust, causing scoped impact: liquidators cannot fully liquidate dust positions, leaving unliquidatable bad debt? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: touchMarket (market parameter setter)] Can any unprivileged user call touchMarket(market) with rcfThreshold == type(uint256).max under the precondition that the market has LLTV < WAD, triggering a market where the RCF dust check is always satisfied (any collateral value < type(uint256).max), meaning the RCF is always deactivated and full liquidations are always allowed, violating the invariant that partial liquidations should restore health for large positions, causing scoped impact: healthy positions can be fully liquidated in one step, bypassing the recovery close factor? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: touchMarket (market parameter setter)] Can any unprivileged user call touchMarket(market) with enterGate == address(0) and liquidatorGate == address(0) under the precondition that the same loanToken has other markets with non-zero gates, triggering creation of an unrestricted market for the same loanToken, violating the invariant that gate restrictions are per-market and cannot be bypassed by creating a new market, causing scoped impact: users restricted by gates in one market can freely enter an unrestricted market with the same loanToken? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee + settlementFee interpolation] Can feeSetter set settlementFeeCbp at breakpoint index=1 (1 day) to a value higher than index=2 (7 days) under the precondition that the piecewise linear interpolation formula uses feeLower and feeUpper without enforcing monotonicity, triggering a non-monotonic fee schedule where TTM between 1-7 days has higher fees than TTM > 7 days, violating the invariant that settlement fees should be monotonically increasing with TTM, causing scoped impact: takers near the 1-7 day TTM range pay unexpectedly high fees, implicitly cancelling their offers? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee + updatePositionView] Can feeSetter call setMarketContinuousFee(id, 0) after lenders have accumulated pendingFee under the precondition that existing lenders have pendingFee > 0 crystallized at the old rate, triggering a continuousFee change to 0 that does NOT reduce existing lenders' pendingFee (fee is fixed at credit creation), violating the invariant that fee changes must not retroactively benefit or harm existing lenders, causing scoped impact: existing lenders continue paying the old fee rate while new lenders pay zero, creating unfair fee treatment? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimContinuousFee + withdraw] Can feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) with amount == withdrawable under the precondition that lenders have credit > 0 and withdrawable == continuousFeeCredit (all withdrawable assets are fee credit), triggering withdrawable to drop to 0, violating the invariant that lenders must be able to withdraw their credit after maturity, causing scoped impact: lenders cannot withdraw any assets post-maturity because withdrawable == 0? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + liquidate] Can an authorized operator call liquidate(market, collateralIndex, seizedAssets, repaidUnits, borrower, postMaturityMode, receiver, callback, data) on behalf of a borrower under the precondition that isAuthorized[borrower][operator] == true and the borrower is healthy, triggering a liquidation attempt that should fail with NotLiquidatable(), violating the invariant that only unhealthy or post-maturity positions are liquidatable, causing scoped impact: authorization does not grant liquidation rights over the authorizer's own position (liquidate is permissionless for the liquidator, not the borrower)? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + repay] Can an authorized operator call repay(market, units, onBehalf=victim, callback=attacker, data) under the precondition that isAuthorized[victim][operator] == true and the callback is attacker-controlled, triggering a repay where the callback (attacker) is the payer and can perform arbitrary actions during onRepay, violating the invariant that repay callbacks must not corrupt partial state, causing scoped impact: attacker's onRepay callback can reenter Midnight to manipulate victim's position before repay completes? [File: 'src/libraries/ConstantsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee + claimSettlementFee accounting] Can feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newFee) to increase the settlement fee, then feeClaimer calls claimSettlementFee(token, amount, receiver) with amount > claimableSettlementFee[token] under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee was not updated by the fee change (only take() updates it), triggering an overclaim attempt, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee must equal the sum of all (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) from all takes, causing scoped impact: fee claimer cannot claim more than actually accrued, but the fee change creates a false expectation of higher claimable fees?
